title: xanthoxalis albicans
intro: Xanthoxalis albicans, also known as Yellow Wood Sorrel or Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) with edible leaves and stems that contain oxalic acid. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce pain and fight off infections. The leaves and stems of the Xanthoxalis albicans plant are said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a medicinal drink, and it can also be used in salads or as a cooking green, but caution should be taken due to its high oxalic acid content.
